steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
Left of the blank: “In many languages, including English, these consonants are” forms the beginning of an independent clause, incomplete without its predicate.

Right of the blank: “in some dialects of Mandarin, however, four such consonants are relatively common” is a complete independent clause (with an introductory prepositional phrase and a parenthetical “however”).

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content:
A semicolon joins two closely related independent clauses, which is what choice D does.

The other choices violate this rule — choice A doesn't create valid coordination (you can't use “and” to join an adjective with a structure starting with a prepositional phrase), choice B leaves a comma splice, and choice C attaches “in some dialects of Mandarin” to the first clause, creating semantic confusion about the contrast being expressed.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
